examples.title: Directory Navigation Script
examples.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Directory Navigation Demo

echo "=== Current Location ==="
pwd
echo ""

echo "=== Create directories ==="
mkdir -p ~/demo/backup ~/demo/source
echo "Created demo folders"
echo ""

echo "=== Navigate using absolute path ==="
cd /home/$USER/demo/source
pwd
echo ""

echo "=== Navigate using relative path ==="
cd ../backup
pwd
echo ""

echo "=== Go to home directory ==="
cd ~
pwd
echo ""

echo "=== Go to previous directory ==="
cd -
pwd
examples.explanation: This script demonstrates essential directory navigation including mkdir, cd with absolute/relative paths, cd ~ for home, and cd - for previous directory.

examples.title: Path Resolution Demonstration
examples.description: Shows the difference between absolute and relative path resolution
examples.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Path Resolution Examples

# Current working directory
CURRENT=$(pwd)
echo "Current directory: $CURRENT"
echo ""

# Example 1: From /home, access /home/user/docs
# Absolute: /home/user/docs
# Relative: ./user/docs (if in /home)
echo "=== Absolute Path ==="
echo "/home/user/docs/file.txt"
echo ""

echo "=== Relative Path (from /home/user) ==="
echo "./docs/file.txt"
echo ""

e# Demonstrating path traversal
echo "=== Path Traversal Demo ==="
echo "From /home/user/project/src"
echo ".. → /home/user/project"
echo "../.. → /home"
echo "../../.. → /"
examples.explanation: Demonstrates how absolute and relative paths are resolved and shows path traversal using .. operator.
